concept: Float / Rest on Water / Be Borne Up
description: To float: the leaf on the pond, the boat at anchor, the swimmer resting. Being borne by water without effort — the still counterpart of 'wishe' (swim) and 'selu' (flow).
sound_symbolism: Light 'lu' rests on the surface, 'pho' is the breath that keeps it there — floating as the water's exhale under a thing.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive: 'lirowa mua melothe lupho.' (The leaf floats on the pond.) At rest: 'mia lupho.' (I float.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Floating is trust demonstrated: the body that struggles sinks, the body that rests is held. Most meditation instruction is this verb.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The ferryman read the river by what floated on it; driftwood and leaf-rafts were the water's own reports.
